meta.notes: AI-generated proof-of-concept CPBS items for SpeedyCAT (chunk 3 of 10). NOT real MCAT/AAMC content and NOT from any answer key. Every stem and choice is plain text (no HTML/SVG/LaTeX). Answer keys were computed programmatically and each item passed a 4-criteria eval (four distinct choices; clean plain-text formatting; MCAT-appropriate difficulty; exactly one correct choice).

questions.stem: What is the approximate molar mass of NaCl? (Use H=1, C=12, N=14, O=16, Na=23, Cl=35.5, S=32, K=39, Ca=40, Mg=24, Fe=56, Ag=108)
questions.explanation: Summing the atomic masses in NaCl gives a molar mass of about 58.5 g/mol.
questions.sourceLicense: AI-generated proof-of-concept — not real MCAT/AAMC content

questions.stem: What is the molarity of a solution with 1 mol of solute in 1 L of solution?
questions.sourceLicense: AI-generated proof-of-concept — not real MCAT/AAMC content

questions.stem: A reaction proceeds in two steps with ΔH1 = -400 kJ and ΔH2 = 250 kJ. If the overall reaction is the sum of these steps, what is the overall ΔH?
questions.explanation: By Hess's law, ΔH_overall = ΔH1 + ΔH2 = -400 + 250 = -150 kJ.
questions.sourceLicense: AI-generated proof-of-concept — not real MCAT/AAMC content

questions.stem: An ideal fluid flows at 3 m/s through a pipe of cross-sectional area 4 m^2. What is the volume flow rate?
questions.explanation: Flow rate Q = A v = (4)(3) = 12 m^3/s.
questions.sourceLicense: AI-generated proof-of-concept — not real MCAT/AAMC content

questions.stem: What is the ground-state electron configuration of a neutral helium (Z=2) atom?
questions.explanation: Filling orbitals by the Aufbau principle gives helium: 1s2.
questions.sourceLicense: AI-generated proof-of-concept — not real MCAT/AAMC content

questions.stem: A net force of 20 N acts on a 3 kg object. What is its acceleration?
questions.sourceLicense: AI-generated proof-of-concept — not real MCAT/AAMC content

questions.stem: How much energy is stored in a 1 μF capacitor charged to 5 V?
questions.sourceLicense: AI-generated proof-of-concept — not real MCAT/AAMC content

questions.stem: After 2 half-lives, what fraction of a radioactive sample remains?
questions.explanation: Each half-life halves the amount: fraction = (1/2)^2 = 0.25.
questions.sourceLicense: AI-generated proof-of-concept — not real MCAT/AAMC content

questions.stem: Metallic bonding is characterized by

choices.label: A
choices.text: positive ions in a sea of delocalized electrons.

choices.label: B
choices.text: electron pairs localized between two atoms.

choices.label: C
choices.text: full transfer of electrons to form a salt.

questions.explanation: In metals, valence electrons are delocalized over a lattice of cations, explaining conductivity.
questions.sourceLicense: AI-generated proof-of-concept — not real MCAT/AAMC content

questions.stem: If 0.02 L of 2 M solution is diluted to 1 L, what is the new concentration?
questions.sourceLicense: AI-generated proof-of-concept — not real MCAT/AAMC content

questions.stem: For a reaction, ΔH = -100 kJ and ΔS = -0.1 kJ/K. What is ΔG at 300 K?
questions.sourceLicense: AI-generated proof-of-concept — not real MCAT/AAMC content

questions.stem: The second law of thermodynamics states that the total entropy of an isolated system
questions.explanation: Spontaneous processes increase the total entropy of an isolated system.
questions.sourceLicense: AI-generated proof-of-concept — not real MCAT/AAMC content

questions.stem: Which atom has the larger atomic radius, Li or K?

choices.label: C
choices.text: atomic radius shows no periodic trend

questions.explanation: K has the larger atomic radius: same group; radius increases down a group, so K (lower) is larger.
questions.sourceLicense: AI-generated proof-of-concept — not real MCAT/AAMC content

questions.stem: What is the weight of a 5 kg object near Earth's surface? (g = 9.8 m/s^2)
questions.sourceLicense: AI-generated proof-of-concept — not real MCAT/AAMC content

questions.stem: How much work is required to move a charge of 2 C through a potential difference of 6 V?
questions.sourceLicense: AI-generated proof-of-concept — not real MCAT/AAMC content

questions.stem: A sample starts with 80 g of a radioisotope whose half-life is 2 days. How much remains after 2 days?
questions.explanation: Number of half-lives = 2/2 = 1; remaining = 80 x (1/2)^1 = 40 g.
questions.sourceLicense: AI-generated proof-of-concept — not real MCAT/AAMC content

questions.stem: According to VSEPR theory, what is the molecular geometry of CH4?
questions.explanation: CH4 adopts a tetrahedral geometry based on its bonding and lone-pair electron domains.
questions.sourceLicense: AI-generated proof-of-concept — not real MCAT/AAMC content

questions.stem: What is the mass of 3 mol of H2O? (molar mass 18 g/mol)
questions.explanation: mass = moles x molar mass = 3 x 18 = 54 g.
questions.sourceLicense: AI-generated proof-of-concept — not real MCAT/AAMC content

questions.stem: A solution has pH = 3. What is its hydrogen ion concentration [H+]?
questions.sourceLicense: AI-generated proof-of-concept — not real MCAT/AAMC content

questions.stem: What is the equilibrium constant expression K for the reaction 2 SO2 + O2 ⇌ 2 SO3?
questions.explanation: K places products over reactants, each concentration raised to its coefficient: K = [SO3]^2 / ([SO2]^2[O2]).
questions.sourceLicense: AI-generated proof-of-concept — not real MCAT/AAMC content

questions.stem: A system absorbs 600 J of heat and does 200 J of work on its surroundings. What is the change in its internal energy?
questions.explanation: First law: ΔU = Q - W = 600 - 200 = 400 J.
questions.sourceLicense: AI-generated proof-of-concept — not real MCAT/AAMC content

questions.stem: Which atom has the higher first ionization energy, Li or Cs?

choices.label: C
choices.text: Ionization energy shows no periodic trend

questions.explanation: Li has the higher first ionization energy: same group; ionization energy decreases down a group, so Li is higher.
questions.sourceLicense: AI-generated proof-of-concept — not real MCAT/AAMC content

questions.stem: What is the momentum of a 3 kg object moving at 20 m/s?
questions.sourceLicense: AI-generated proof-of-concept — not real MCAT/AAMC content

questions.stem: What happens between two objects carrying opposite electric charges?

choices.label: B
choices.text: They always share charge equally at a distance.

choices.label: D
choices.text: They exert no force on each other.

questions.explanation: Opposite charges attract; like charges repel.
questions.sourceLicense: AI-generated proof-of-concept — not real MCAT/AAMC content

questions.stem: Sound waves in air are
questions.explanation: Sound is a longitudinal pressure wave: the medium compresses and rarefies along the travel direction.
questions.sourceLicense: AI-generated proof-of-concept — not real MCAT/AAMC content

questions.stem: What is the approximate bond angle in CH4 (tetrahedral)?
questions.explanation: A tetrahedral molecule such as CH4 has bond angles of 109.5 degrees.
questions.sourceLicense: AI-generated proof-of-concept — not real MCAT/AAMC content

questions.stem: How many molecules are in 0.5 mol of a substance? (Avogadro's number = 6.022 x 10^23 /mol)
questions.explanation: molecules = moles x N_A = 0.5 x 6.022 x 10^23 = 3.01 x 10^23.
questions.sourceLicense: AI-generated proof-of-concept — not real MCAT/AAMC content

questions.stem: What is the pH of a solution with [H+] = 1 x 10^-3 M?
questions.sourceLicense: AI-generated proof-of-concept — not real MCAT/AAMC content

questions.stem: According to Le Chatelier's principle, adding more product to a system at equilibrium generally

choices.label: A
choices.text: shifts toward the side with more moles of gas.

choices.label: B
choices.text: causes no shift in the position of equilibrium.

choices.label: C
choices.text: shifts the equilibrium toward the products.

choices.label: D
choices.text: shifts the equilibrium toward the reactants.

questions.explanation: The system opposes the change: adding more product to a system at equilibrium shifts the equilibrium toward the reactants.
questions.sourceLicense: AI-generated proof-of-concept — not real MCAT/AAMC content

questions.stem: A gas expands by 0.01 m^3 against a constant pressure of 2 x 10^5 Pa. How much work does it do?
questions.sourceLicense: AI-generated proof-of-concept — not real MCAT/AAMC content

questions.stem: Isotopes of an element have the same number of

choices.label: A
choices.text: electrons but different numbers of protons.

choices.label: C
choices.text: protons but different numbers of neutrons.

choices.label: D
choices.text: neutrons but different numbers of protons.

questions.explanation: Isotopes share atomic number but differ in neutron count and thus mass number.
questions.sourceLicense: AI-generated proof-of-concept — not real MCAT/AAMC content

questions.sourceLicense: AI-generated proof-of-concept — not real MCAT/AAMC content

questions.stem: A current of 0.2 A flows through a 10 Ω resistor. What is the voltage across it?
questions.explanation: Ohm's law: V = I R = (0.2)(10) = 2 V.

questions.stem: What is the hybridization of the central atom in C2H4 (ethene)?

questions.stem: For the reaction C3H8 + 5 O2 → 3 CO2 + 4 H2O, how many moles of CO2 form from 2 mol of C3H8 (with the other reactant in excess)?

questions.stem: The bond in H2 has an electronegativity difference of about 0. It is best classified as
questions.explanation: An electronegativity difference near 0 indicates a nonpolar covalent bond (roughly: 0 nonpolar covalent, up to about 1.7 polar covalent, larger values ionic).

questions.stem: What is the mass percent of C in CO2?
questions.explanation: mass percent = (mass of element / molar mass) x 100 = (12/44) x 100 = 27.3%.

questions.stem: A gas at 5 atm occupies 1 L. At constant temperature, what volume does it occupy at 1 atm?
questions.explanation: Boyle's law: P1 V1 = P2 V2, so V2 = (5)(1)/1 = 5 L.
